01_initial_request.input_data: Escherichia coli host system engineered with thermal-responsive repressor cI857 and MutS mutant, containing initial enzyme genes or biosynthetic pathways
01_initial_request.user_intent: Develop an ultrahigh throughput in vivo continuous directed evolution system for the rapid evolution of enzymes and biosynthetic pathways to overcome labor- and time-intensive limitations of classical directed evolution
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: 48.3% improvement in α-amylase activity and 1.7-fold higher resveratrol production

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Classical directed evolution relies on labor-intensive iterative cycles with limited screening throughput. [Gap] Need a dynamic regulatory element with rapid responsiveness for regulatable in vivo evolution system. [Decision] To achieve efficient thermal-responsive control, chose to use GeneMorph II Random Mutagenesis Kit to perform random mutagenesis on cI857 repressor gene, expecting to obtain a library of cI857 mutants with improved temperature sensitivity.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: cI857 random mutant library constructed and assembled into plasmid pRSFDuet-PasnS-cI857

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] cI857 library constructed with EGFP expression controlled by PR promoter. [Gap] Need to select mutants with higher induction at 37°C. [Decision] To achieve positive screening for thermal-responsive switch, chose to use FACSAria III flow cytometer to perform first round FACS sorting at 37°C, expecting to obtain cells with higher fluorescence indicating stronger induction.

parameters.sorting_threshold: top 0.3% of cells with highest fluorescence intensity

02_agent_trajectory.observation: At least 10,000 events with higher fluorescence collected through first round of FACS

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] First round FACS completed to select high fluorescence at 37°C. [Gap] Need to reduce expression leakage at repressive temperature (30°C). [Decision] To achieve low-leakage mutants, chose to use FACSAria III flow cytometer to perform second round FACS sorting at 30°C, expecting to obtain cells with lower fluorescence indicating reduced leakage.

parameters.sorting_threshold: bottom 0.4% of cells with lowest fluorescence intensity

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Approximately 5,000 events showing lower fluorescence sorted from collected cells cultured at 30°C

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Two rounds of FACS completed to select cI857 mutants. [Gap] Need to characterize fluorescence intensity of selected clones at different temperatures. [Decision] To achieve final mutant selection, chose to use microplate reader to perform fluorescence assay on 768 strains at 30°C and 37°C, expecting to identify mutant M1 with optimal thermal response.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Evolved mutant M1 isolated with lower fluorescence at 30°C and higher fluorescence at 37°C; fluorescent protein expression at 37°C was 134.8 times that at 30°C

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] cI857* mutant identified with improved thermal response. [Gap] Need to construct temperature-controlled in vivo evolution system with Pol I*. [Decision] To achieve targeted mutagenesis system, chose to use Gibson Assembly to construct two-plasmid system with Pol I* under PR promoter control, expecting to obtain mutator plasmid pSC101 and target plasmid pET28a.

parameters.mutator_plasmid: pSC101 with pol I* under PR promoter

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Two-plasmid system constructed with Pol I* expression controlled by thermal-responsive PR promoter

references.citation: Camps M, Naukkarinen J, Johnson BP, Loeb LA. 2003
references.relevance: Previous report on highly mutagenic DNA polymerase I for targeted gene evolution

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Two-plasmid system constructed for in vivo mutagenesis. [Gap] Need to quantify targeted mutation rate. [Decision] To achieve mutation rate measurement, chose to use LB plates with antibiotics to perform carbenicillin-resistance reversion assay, expecting to obtain mutation frequency data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Strain containing Pol I* plasmid showed significantly lower reversion rate at 30°C than at 37°C; highest reversion rate at 250 bp from ColE1 ori, approximately 41 times that of strain with Pol I plasmid

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Pol I* system characterized with temperature control. [Gap] Need to further elevate mutation rate by modifying MMR system. [Decision] To achieve enhanced mutagenesis, chose to use CRISPR-Cas9 system to introduce mutS A134V mutation into E. coli BL21 (DE3) genome, expecting to obtain temperature-sensitive MutS60 strain.

parameters.elimination_method: 0.5 mmol/L IPTG at 30°C and serial passaging

02_agent_trajectory.observation: MutS60 strain obtained with temperature-sensitive defect in mismatch repair

references.citation: Jiang Y, Chen B, Duan C, Sun B, Yang J, Yang S. 2015
references.relevance: CRISPR-Cas9 system for multigene editing in E. coli genome

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] MutS60 strain constructed. [Gap] Need to characterize combined effect of Pol I* and MutS60 on mutation rate. [Decision] To achieve optimal mutagenesis conditions, chose to use LB plates with antibiotics to perform reversion rate assay at different temperatures, expecting to identify optimal temperature for targeted mutagenesis.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: At 43°C, MutS60 strain with Pol I* gene showed optimal plasmid mutation rate, approximately 638 times that of wild-type strain with Pol I at 250 bp; mutation rate determined to be 6.77×10^-8 per nucleotide per cell per generation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] In vivo mutagenesis system optimized with Pol I* and MutS60. [Gap] Need to apply system to α-amylase evolution. [Decision] To achieve α-amylase surface display, chose to use Gibson Assembly to construct BLA fusion ORF with Lpp-OmpA under rhaBAD promoter, expecting to obtain BLA-ori plasmid for evolution.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: BLA activity of cells much higher than supernatant, indicating successful surface expression

references.citation: Yang WH, Li MJ, Wu MQ, Yu SQ, Zhou JW. 2022
references.relevance: Surface display method for enzyme engineering

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] BLA-ori plasmid constructed for evolution. [Gap] Need to generate mutant library through iterative mutagenesis. [Decision] To achieve continuous evolution, chose to use temperature cycling to perform mutagenesis-enrichment process, expecting to enrich mutants with increased BLA enzyme activity.

parameters.medium: 2*YT for mutagenesis, enrichment medium with 2% starch

02_agent_trajectory.observation: OD600 of cultures after 1 round significantly higher than control, reaching up to 1.5 at 30h; growth rate showed gradual increasing trend with consecutive passages

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 25 rounds of mutagenesis-enrichment completed for BLA library. [Gap] Need to screen high-activity variants from large library. [Decision] To achieve ultrahigh-throughput screening, chose to use microfluidic droplet generator to perform single-cell encapsulation and sorting, expecting to isolate variants with increased α-amylase activity.

parameters.sorting_threshold: 0.1% of droplets with fluorescence signals exceeding threshold

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 72% of sorted cells showed over 20% increased activity compared with wild-type strain

references.citation: Agresti JJ, Antipov E, Abate AR, et al. 2010
references.relevance: Ultrahigh-throughput screening in drop-based microfluidics for directed evolution

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] High-activity BLA variants sorted by microfluidics. [Gap] Need to quantify enzyme activity improvement. [Decision] To achieve activity validation, chose to use DNS method to perform α-amylase activity assay, expecting to confirm activity improvement of mutant BLA (N473Y).

parameters.mutation: N473Y at 217 bp downstream of ColE1 ori

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Fermentation activity of retransformed strain carrying mutant BLA (N473Y) increased by 48.3%

references.citation: Zhang G, Chen Y, Li Q, Zhou J, Li J, Du G. 2021
references.relevance: Growth-coupled evolution and high-throughput screening for amylase enhancement

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] α-amylase evolution successful. [Gap] Need to apply system to resveratrol biosynthetic pathway. [Decision] To achieve resveratrol detection, chose to use Gibson Assembly to construct TtgR-based biosensor plasmid pTtgR with mCherry reporter, expecting to obtain fluorescence response to resveratrol.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Biosensor showed excellent specificity for resveratrol with much lower induction response to p-coumaric acid

references.citation: Terán W, Felipe A, Segura A, et al. 2003
references.relevance: TtgR transcriptional regulatory system from Pseudomonas putida

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Resveratrol biosensor constructed. [Gap] Need to construct biosynthetic pathway for evolution. [Decision] To achieve resveratrol production, chose to use Gibson Assembly to construct pETDuet plasmid with STS and 4CL genes, expecting to obtain resveratrol biosynthesis pathway.

parameters.enzymes: 4CL1 from Arabidopsis thaliana, STS from Vitis vinifera

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Resveratrol titer approximately 160 mg/L after 28h shake flask fermentation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Resveratrol pathway and biosensor constructed. [Gap] Need to generate mutant library for pathway evolution. [Decision] To achieve continuous evolution of pathway, chose to use temperature cycling to perform iterative mutagenesis passages, expecting to enrich high-producing strains.

parameters.strain: MM strain (MutS60 with Pol I*, pathway, and biosensor)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Mutagenesis library constructed through iterative mutagenesis; biosensor stability confirmed after 7 and 16 passages

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 16 rounds of mutagenesis completed for resveratrol pathway. [Gap] Need to screen high-producing variants from library. [Decision] To achieve ultrahigh-throughput screening, chose to use flow cytometry to perform FACS sorting based on mCherry fluorescence, expecting to isolate strains with improved resveratrol production.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Over 60% of sorted cells exhibited improved fluorescence intensity compared with starting strain

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] High-fluorescence strains sorted by FACS. [Gap] Need to quantify resveratrol production improvement. [Decision] To achieve production validation, chose to use Prominence LC-20A HPLC to perform resveratrol titer measurement, expecting to confirm production improvement of selected strains.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Resveratrol titer of selected strains was more than twice that of control group; variant with 1.7-fold higher resveratrol production identified

references.citation: Liu M, Wang C, Ren X, Gao S, Yu S, Zhou J. 2022

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] High-producing strains identified with 1.7-fold improvement. [Gap] Need to understand molecular basis of improvement. [Decision] To achieve mechanism validation, chose to use RT-qPCR to perform mRNA level analysis of STS gene, expecting to determine if mutations affect transcription.

parameters.plasmid_mutations: ColE1 ori C148T and Δ5526-6717 deletion

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Relative mRNA level of STS in retransformed strains harboring mpETDuet was approximately 4 times higher than wild type; Δ5526-6717 deletion contributes greatly to enhancement

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Enzyme activity assay (DNS method) and HPLC analysis for metabolite production

alpha_amylase_activity_improvement.interpretation: Improvement in α-amylase activity achieved through microfluidic droplet screening after 25 rounds of mutagenesis-enrichment

resveratrol_production_improvement.interpretation: Higher resveratrol production achieved through FACS biosensor screening after 16 rounds of mutagenesis

mutation_rate_increase.interpretation: Targeted mutation rate increased compared to background mutation rate in E. coli through Pol I* and MutS60 combination

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was successfully achieved. The ultrahigh-throughput in vivo continuous directed evolution system was developed and demonstrated with both α-amylase (48.3% activity improvement) and resveratrol biosynthetic pathway (1.7-fold production increase), meeting the quantifiable goals specified in the research objective.
